skill.name: scaffold-exercises
skill.description: Create exercise directory structures with sections, problems, solutions, and explainers that pass linting. Use when user wants to scaffold exercises, create exercise stubs, or set up a new course section.
skill.summary: Scaffold exercise directory structures for courses with lint-validated sections, problems, solutions, and explainers.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged 47 potential issues: 44 'external_commands' and 3 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns. After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' detections are documentation examples (inline shell commands in markdown backticks) instructing the AI to run legitimate CLI tools like `pnpm ai-hero-cli internal lint`, `git commit`, `git mv`, and `mkdir -p`. The 'weak crypto' detections on lines 3, 34, and 39 are matches on innocuous text (the word 'description' and example template content), not actual cryptographic operations. The skill is a benign course-scaffolding guide with no data exfiltration, network calls, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm pattern (lines 3, 34, 39)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged lines 3, 34, and 39 for 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. Evaluation: These lines contain the frontmatter 'description' field, the word 'description' in instructional text, and 'Description here' in a markdown code block example. No actual cryptographic operations exist in this file. All three detections are false positives from regex matching on the word 'description' or similar text.

content.value_statement: Creating exercise directory structures for courses is repetitive and error-prone. This skill automates scaffolding sections, problems, solutions, and explainers that pass linting on the first try.
actual_capabilities: Create exercise directory structures with numbered section and exercise prefixes
Generate problem, solution, and explainer subfolders per exercise
Stub readme.md files with titles and descriptions for each subfolder
Run pnpm ai-hero-cli internal lint to validate the structure
Fix lint errors iteratively until validation passes
Rename and renumber exercise directories using git mv to preserve history
limitations: Only supports the ai-hero-cli linting workflow and directory conventions
Cannot generate actual exercise content beyond stub readmes
Requires manual provision of a course plan with section and exercise names
No support for non-dash-case naming or alternative folder structures

use_cases.title: Course author bootstraps a new section
use_cases.description: An instructor with a course plan asks the skill to create all exercise directories, subfolders, and stub readmes for a new section, then runs the linter to confirm structure.

use_cases.title: Teaching assistant restructures existing exercises
use_cases.description: A teaching assistant renumbers and moves exercise directories using git mv to preserve history, then re-runs the linter to ensure compliance.

use_cases.title: Developer follows convention for personal tutorials
use_cases.description: An independent developer uses the skill to scaffold a tutorial repository that follows the same lint-validated exercise structure used in their team's courses.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a single section from a plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold section 05-memory-skill-building with three exercises: 05.01 introduction (explainer only), 05.02 short-term memory (explainer, problem, solution), 05.03 long-term memory (explainer only).
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a new section from a written plan

prompt_templates.title: Generate stub readmes for all variants
prompt_templates.prompt: For exercises/05-memory-skill-building, create stub readme.md files in each subfolder with the exercise title as a level-1 heading.

prompt_templates.title: Renumber and move an exercise
prompt_templates.prompt: Move exercises/01-retrieval/01.03-embeddings to exercises/01-retrieval/01.04-embeddings using git mv, then re-run the linter.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reordering exercises while preserving git history

prompt_templates.title: Full scaffold and lint workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse this plan, create all directories with mkdir -p, write stub readmes, run pnpm ai-hero-cli internal lint, and fix any errors until lint passes.

output_examples.input: Scaffold section 05 with 05.01 introduction (explainer), 05.02 short-term memory (explainer, problem, solution), 05.03 long-term memory (explainer).
output: Wrote stub readme.md in each subfolder with exercise title
Ran pnpm ai-hero-cli internal lint — passed

best_practices: Always use git mv when renaming or renumbering exercise directories to preserve git history
Default to explainer-only stubs unless the plan specifies problem or solution variants
Re-run the linter after any structural change to catch broken links or missing files
anti_patterns: Using plain mv instead of git mv when moving exercise directories, which loses history
Committing speaker-notes.md or .gitkeep files inside exercise subfolders, which the linter rejects
Embedding pnpm run exercise commands inside readme.md files, which violates lint rules

faq.question: What linting tool does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill uses pnpm ai-hero-cli internal lint, which checks for proper subfolders, non-empty readmes, no broken links, and other structural rules.

faq.question: Can this skill generate full exercise content?
faq.answer: No. The skill creates directory structures and stub readmes. Authors must provide the actual exercise content.

faq.question: How are exercises numbered?
faq.answer: Sections use a two-digit prefix like 05- and exercises use a dotted prefix like 05.01-. Names must be dash-case.

faq.question: What variants can an exercise have?
faq.answer: An exercise can have problem, solution, and explainer subfolders. At least one is required, and explainer is the default for stubs.

faq.question: How do I rename or renumber exercises safely?
faq.answer: Use git mv to rename directories so git history is preserved, then re-run the linter to confirm the new structure passes.

faq.question: What files are forbidden in exercise subfolders?
faq.answer: The linter rejects .gitkeep files, speaker-notes.md files, and readmes that contain pnpm run exercise commands or broken links.
